Denver, Noise exposure areas around Stapleton and Buckley airports, public housing programs : environmental impact statement.

The City of Aurora, Colorado, is proposing to implement various federally funded housing programs in areas around Stapleton and Buckley airports that are exposed to high levels of noise. These programs aim to rehabilitate existing homes, provide rent subsidies, and purchase houses for qualifying individuals, but do not include new housing construction. The goal is to maintain neighborhood stability and improve living conditions despite the noise, which remains 'unacceptable' outdoors according to HUD standards. The initiative is expected to create jobs and reduce energy consumption, but may also lead to some dislocation as new residents move in.
Aurora, Adams and Arapahoe Counties, Colorado
City of Aurora Department of Planning and Community Development
Department of Housing and Urban Development

The proposal for housing programs around Stapleton and Buckley airports was initiated in response to the high noise levels affecting the quality of life for residents in these areas. There was some public concern regarding the potential for dislocation as new residents moved in, but the overall aim was to improve living conditions and maintain neighborhood stability. The project sought to address the challenges posed by noise pollution while providing essential housing support to the community.
United States. Department of Housing and Urban Development
"The dislocation of families from this area because of federally sponsored policies of demolition or benign neglect would cause massive social hardships and suffering." — Social Environment Section

"The City housing programs will have a highly favorable impact on the cultural resources in Aurora. The upgrading of housing stock in this area will help the buildings which may be eligible, either individually or collectively, for inclusion in the National Register of Historic Places." — Cultural Resources Section
